questions.question: What types of events do you photograph?
questions.answer: Concerts and festivals, corporate conferences, brand & sponsor activations, ribbon cuttings and grand openings, and graduation/commencement. We're the house photographer for the St. Augustine Amphitheatre and the main photographer for Sing Out Loud and Fort Mose.

questions.question: How much does event photography cost?
questions.answer: Photo coverage starts at $1,200 for a half-day with 100+ edited images; combined photo + video event coverage starts at $5,500. A quick call gets you an exact number.

questions.question: How soon do I get my photos?
questions.answer: Within 72 hours by default, and same-day when the moment needs it — we turned Walk-On's grand opening around same day and edit festivals live so they post that night.

questions.question: Do you shoot photo and video together?
questions.answer: Yes — much of our event work is both, one team on stills and video so you're not juggling vendors.

questions.question: Can you photograph brand and sponsor activations?
questions.answer: Yes. Activation coverage built for sponsorship ROI — the brand in use, attendee engagement, crowd scale — for recap decks, social, and next-year pitches.

questions.question: Do you travel outside St. Augustine?
questions.answer: Yes. We cover events across Northeast Florida and travel nationally — we've photographed the RSA Conference in San Francisco for six years.

questions.question: Are you insured, and can you provide a COI?
questions.answer: Yes — $1M general liability, with a certificate of insurance for your venue, including additional insured if required.

questions.question: Who owns the photos and how can we use them?
questions.answer: Full usage rights (perpetual license across web, print, social, and ads). First Sight Films retains ownership; we deliver edited high-res images, not raw.

questions.question: What areas do you serve?
questions.answer: Based in St. Augustine, serving Jacksonville, Ponte Vedra, St. Johns County, Palm Coast, and Northeast Florida.
